benefitsPage.title: Your Benefits at a Glance
benefitsPage.description: Explore the complete range of benefits available to Premier America team members and their families.

benefitChapters.title: Overview of Available Plans
benefitChapters.description: A summary of all benefit plans available to Premier America employees.

children.text: Premier America offers a comprehensive suite of benefits designed to protect you and your family. Review and compare all available plans below.
Medical Plans

Medical benefits are provided through Aetna and include access to a broad network of physicians, hospitals, and specialists. All plans include preventive care at no cost, telehealth services, and prescription drug coverage.
3 medical plans available: HMO Plan, PPO Plan, and High Deductible Health Plan (HDHP) with HSA
Dental Plans

Dental coverage is provided through Delta Dental and includes diagnostic, preventive, basic, and major services. Using an in-network provider saves you the most money. Visit www.deltadental.com to find an in-network dentist near you.
2 dental plans available: Basic Plan and Premium Plan
Vision Plans

Vision coverage is provided through EyeMed and includes annual eye exams, lenses, frames, and contact lenses. You'll save the most by visiting an in-network  provider. Discounts on LASIK surgery are also available.
1 vision plan available: EyeMed Select

tables.tableDescription: Bi-weekly employee payroll contributions for medical coverage.
Bi-weekly employee payroll contributions for dental coverage.
Bi-weekly employee payroll contributions for vision coverage.

benefitChapters.title: Eligibility & Qualifying Life Events
benefitChapters.description: Who is eligible for benefits, dependent coverage, and qualifying life event information.

children.text: All regular full-time and part-time employees working 30 or more hours per week are eligible for Premier America benefits. Coverage begins on the first of the month following your date of hire.
Eligible Employees
Full-time and part-time employees scheduled to work 30 or more hours per week are eligible for the full benefits package. Seasonal and temporary employees are not eligible.
Eligible Dependents
Legal spouse or domestic partner.
Children up to age 26 (including biological, adopted, step-children, and children placed for adoption). Disabled dependents over age 26 who are incapable of self-support may also be eligible.
When to Enroll
New Hire Enrollment: You must enroll within 31 days of your hire date.
Open Enrollment: Held annually, typically in November for a January 1 effective date.
Qualifying Life Event: You have 31 days from the date of the event to make changes.
Common Qualifying Life Events
Marriage, divorce, or legal separation
Birth, adoption, or placement for adoption of a child
Loss or gain of other coverage (e.g., spouse's employer plan)
Death of a spouse or dependent
Change in employment status that affects eligibility

benefitChapters.title: Medical Plans
benefitChapters.description: Detailed comparison of HMO, PPO, and HDHP medical plan options provided through Aetna.

children.text: Premier America offers three medical plan options through Aetna, each designed to meet different needs and budgets. All plans include 100% coverage for in-network preventive care, access to telehealth, and comprehensive prescription drug benefits.
HMO Plan

The HMO plan offers the lowest out-of-pocket costs with a focus on in-network care. You'll select a Primary Care Physician (PCP) who coordinates your care and provides referrals to specialists. This plan is ideal if you prefer predictable costs and are comfortable staying within the Aetna network.
PPO Plan

The PPO plan provides greater flexibility to see any provider without a referral. You'll pay less when you use in-network providers, but you also have coverage for out-of-network providers at higher cost-sharing. This plan is ideal if you value choice and flexibility in your healthcare.
HDHP with HSA

The High Deductible Health Plan is paired with a Health Savings Account (HSA). It has lower premiums and higher deductibles. Premier America contributes $750 (individual) or $1,500 (family) annually to your HSA. HSA funds roll over year to year and are yours to keep. This plan is ideal for generally healthy individuals who want to save on premiums and build tax-advantaged savings for healthcare.

tables.tableDescription: Side-by-side comparison of key plan features.

benefitChapters.title: Dental Plans
benefitChapters.description: Comparison of Basic and Premium dental plan options through Delta Dental.

children.text: Premier America offers two dental plans through Delta Dental. Both plans cover preventive care, basic services, and major services. Using in-network providers helps you maximize your benefits and minimize out-of-pocket costs.
Basic Plan

The Basic plan covers preventive and diagnostic services at 100% with no deductible. Basic services like fillings are covered at 80%, and major services like crowns and bridges are covered at 50% after meeting your annual deductible.
Premium Plan

The Premium plan offers enhanced coverage with higher annual maximums and orthodontia coverage for children and adults. Preventive care is covered at 100%, basic services at 90%, and major services at 60%. Orthodontia is covered at 50% with a $2,000 lifetime maximum.

tables.tableDescription: Side-by-side comparison of dental plan features.

benefitChapters.title: Life & Disability Insurance
benefitChapters.description: Company-paid and voluntary life insurance, plus short-term and long-term disability coverage through Lincoln Financial.

children.text: Premier America provides company-paid life and disability insurance to help protect you and your family in the event of illness, injury, or death. Additional voluntary coverage is available at group rates.
Company-Paid Basic Life & AD&D

Premier America provides all eligible employees with Basic Life Insurance and Accidental Death & Dismemberment (AD&D) coverage equal to 1.5x your annual base salary, up to a maximum of $500,000. This benefit is provided at no cost to you.
Voluntary Supplemental Life Insurance

You may purchase additional life insurance for yourself (up to $500,000), your spouse (up to $250,000), and your children ($10,000 per child). Guaranteed issue amounts are available during initial enrollment without medical evidence of insurability.
Short-Term Disability (STD)

Company-paid STD provides 60% of your weekly base earnings (up to $1,500/week) for up to 26 weeks if you are unable to work due to a non-work-related illness or injury. Benefits begin after a 7-day elimination period for illness and day 1 for accidents.
Long-Term Disability (LTD)

Company-paid LTD provides 60% of your monthly base earnings (up to $10,000/month) if you remain disabled beyond the STD benefit period. LTD benefits begin after 180 days of disability and can continue until age 65, depending on your age at the time of disability.

tables.tableDescription: Overview of company-paid and voluntary coverage.

benefitChapters.title: Flexible Spending Accounts
benefitChapters.description: Healthcare FSA and Dependent Care FSA information, contribution limits, and eligible expenses.

children.text: Flexible Spending Accounts (FSAs) allow you to set aside pre-tax dollars to pay for eligible healthcare and dependent care expenses. By using pre-tax dollars, you reduce your taxable income and save money on expenses you're already paying for.
Healthcare FSA

The Healthcare FSA lets you contribute up to $3,200 per year in pre-tax dollars to pay for eligible medical, dental, and vision expenses not covered by your insurance. This includes copays, deductibles, prescription costs, and many over-the-counter items. Your full annual election is available on day one of the plan year.
Note: If you enroll in the HDHP with HSA, you may only enroll in a Limited Purpose FSA, which covers only dental and vision expenses.
Dependent Care FSA

The Dependent Care FSA lets you contribute up to $5,000 per year ($2,500 if married filing separately) in pre-tax dollars to pay for eligible dependent care expenses. This includes daycare, preschool, before/after school programs, and elder care for dependents who live with you. Unlike the Healthcare FSA, funds are only available as contributions are made through payroll deductions.

tables.tableDescription: Key features of Healthcare and Dependent Care FSAs.

rows.label: Eligible Expenses
cells: Medical, dental, vision copays, deductibles, prescriptions, OTC items
Daycare, preschool, before/after school care, elder care

openEnrollment.title: 2026 Open Enrollment
openEnrollment.description: Open Enrollment for the 2026 plan year. Review your options and make your elections before the deadline.
openEnrollment.statusDescription: Make your benefit elections for the 2026 plan year.
openEnrollment.checklistSubtext: Follow these steps to complete your enrollment
openEnrollment.changesSubtext: Review the benefit changes for the upcoming year

benefitChangesPage.title: 2026 Benefit Changes

children.text: Premier America continually reviews its benefits offerings to ensure competitive, comprehensive coverage for all employees. Below are the key changes for the 2026 plan year.
Important: Please review all changes carefully during the Open Enrollment period. Elections cannot be changed until the next Open Enrollment unless you experience a qualifying life event.

For 2026, Premier America is introducing a High Deductible Health Plan (HDHP) paired with a Health Savings Account (HSA). The company will contribute $750 (individual) or $1,500 (family) annually to your HSA. This plan offers the lowest monthly premiums and allows you to build tax-advantaged savings for current and future healthcare expenses.

changes.title: Enhanced Dental Premium Plan — Orthodontia Added

children.text: The Premium Dental plan now includes orthodontia coverage for both children and adults at 50% coinsurance with a $2,000 lifetime maximum. This enhancement provides significant savings for families needing braces or other orthodontic treatment.

Premier America has increased its annual HSA seed contribution from $500/$1,000 to $750/$1,500 (individual/family) for employees enrolled in the HDHP. This increase helps offset the higher deductible and provides additional tax-free funds for healthcare expenses.

All medical plans now include enhanced telehealth benefits with 24/7 access to board-certified physicians, mental health counselors, and dermatologists. HMO members pay just $10 per visit, PPO members $20, and HDHP members pay 20% after deductible.

benefitChangesPage.ctaDescription: Visit the enrollment portal to make your benefit elections for 2026.

enrollmentChecklist.title: Enrollment Checklist

children.text: Follow these steps to ensure a smooth and complete benefits enrollment for the 2026 plan year.

Take a look at your current benefits elections and consider whether they still meet your needs. Think about any life changes that occurred this year and how they might affect your coverage needs for 2026.

Check the Benefit Changes page to learn about new plan options, coverage improvements, and any cost changes. Pay particular attention to the new HDHP/HSA option and the enhanced dental orthodontia coverage.

You'll need Social Security numbers and dates of birth for any dependents you plan to enroll. If adding a new dependent, have a copy of your marriage certificate, birth certificate, or adoption paperwork available.

Log in to the enrollment portal at enrollment.premieramerica.com and complete your benefit elections. Be sure to designate your beneficiaries and review your elections summary before submitting. Print or save a confirmation for your records.

enrollmentChecklist.ctaDescription: Contact the HR Benefits Team at benefits@premieramerica.com or call 1-800-555-9999 for assistance with your enrollment.

children.text: Premier America is committed to helping you build a secure financial future. Take advantage of our retirement savings plans with generous company matching to grow your nest egg.

Premier America offers a 401(k) plan through Fidelity Investments. You can contribute up to the IRS annual limit ($23,500 for 2026, plus an additional $7,500 catch-up contribution if you're age 50 or older). Both pre-tax (Traditional) and after-tax (Roth) contribution options are available.

Company Match: Premier America matches 100% of the first 4% of your salary that you contribute, plus 50% of the next 2%. That's a maximum company match of 5% of your eligible compensation. You are immediately vested in your own contributions, and company matching contributions vest over 3 years (33% per year).

The 401(k) plan offers a diversified menu of investment options including target-date funds, index funds, actively managed funds, and a self-directed brokerage option. All funds have been carefully selected by our Investment Committee and are reviewed quarterly.
If you don't make an investment election, your contributions will be automatically invested in the target-date fund closest to your expected retirement year based on age 65.

Premier America provides access to free financial planning tools and resources through Fidelity, including one-on-one consultations with financial advisors, retirement readiness assessments, online calculators, and educational webinars covering topics like debt management, saving for college, and Social Security planning.
